key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 cash from operations $25.68M minus capital spending $0.93M, divided by 99.23M diluted weighted-average shares (10-K filed 2026-03-13)
Life Solutions origination scale + asset-management fee growth; securitization lowers funding cost
100% Marvin GAAP floor (no approved third party)

open_questions: Correct onboard `croupier` tag to **platform** (life settlements, not exchange)
Management alignment and roll-up underwriting quality: no fieldwork
Whether adjusted EBITDA should ever inform owner-cash normalization for stance gate
Dividend sustainability at $0.20/sh vs. lumpy GAAP cash flow
thesis: Abacus Global Management (NYSE: ABX; notes: ABXL) is an alternative-asset and life-settlement platform. **[Fact]** The company originates and brokers purchases of life insurance policies between policy sellers and institutional buyers, services those policies until mortality, and earns spread plus fees. **[Fact]** It also runs asset-management sleeves (including free-cash-flow-themed strategies via FCF Advisors, acquired December 2024) and a small technology services line (Abacus Intel mortality verification, launched late 2024). **[Fact]** Revenue is reported in three segments: Life Solutions, Asset Management, and Technology Services. **[Fact]** The company renamed from Abacus Life, Inc. in February 2025 and lists on the NYSE with 9.875% senior notes due 2028 (ABXL).
why_market_wrong: Life-settlement economics are opaque: GAAP net income can look modest while adjusted earnings and policy-portfolio marks tell a stronger story. **[Inference]** FY2025 revenue rose to $235 million (+110% year over year) and adjusted EBITDA reached $133 million, yet FY2025 cash from operations was only $26 million because policy purchases and working capital absorb cash before mortality payoffs arrive. **[Inference]** The stock near $9.25 (~2.2 times GAAP book) may screen expensive on trailing GAAP free cash flow while underpricing a rollup that doubled revenue through Carlisle and FCF Advisors. **[Inference]** Complexity (fair-value policy portfolio, securitized funding, dual equity/note structure) may keep generalist investors away even when servicing fees and securitization are repeatable.
